lines.name: Vienna — Classical 3.Bc4
lines.idea: Calm classical setup: develop the bishop to c4 eyeing f7, support e4 with d3, and route the knight via e2 so the c-pawn and f-pawn stay free. Castle and play for f4 later.

lines.name: Vienna Gambit — Accepted
lines.idea: The Vienna Gambit: 3.f4 strikes at e5. After ...exf4 the e5 push gains time on the f6-knight, and Qe2 holds the centre. White trades the f4-pawn back later with active piece play down the f-file.

lines.name: Vienna Gambit — Declined with 3...d5
lines.idea: Black's best reply 3...d5 counterstrikes in the centre. Take on e5, let Black grab e4, then build a broad pawn centre with Nf3 and d4. White has a healthy space advantage and the e5-pawn cramps Black.

lines.name: Vienna Gambit — 3...exf4 sideline
lines.idea: If Black tries to cling to the f4-pawn with ...g5, undermine the pawn chain with h4. The knight jumps to g5 hitting f7; Black must spend time defending while White's attack rolls on the kingside.

lines.name: Vienna — 2...Nc6 with Bc4
lines.idea: Against the natural 2...Nc6 3...Bc5, the aggressive Qg4 hits g7 and pressures f7. Nd5 piles in with threats on c7 and f6. A sharp tactical line where White seizes the initiative early.

lines.name: Vienna — 3.g3 fianchetto
lines.idea: A quieter positional try: fianchetto the bishop to g2 to pressure the long diagonal and the d5/e4 squares. Castle short, complete development with Nge2, and prepare d3 and f4 for a slow kingside expansion.
